title: Drepanophyllum luteum
taxonomy: Drepanophyllum luteum is a type of flowering plant. It belongs to the family of plants known as Zingiberaceae. This family is commonly referred to as the ginger family, as it includes several species of ginger plants. Drepanophyllum luteum is native to tropical regions of Asia and is often cultivated for its edible rhizomes. Its classification is further categorized under the genus Drepanophyllum, which is a part of the Zingiberaceae family.
uses: Drepanophyllum luteum is a species of plant that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, it has been used to treat fever, cough, and cold.
